Home
Guide

# is-extglob (opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window)

Returns true if a string has an extglob.

# Install

Install with npm (opens new window):

$ npm install --save is-extglob

# Usage

var isExtglob = require('is-extglob');

True

isExtglob('?(abc)');
isExtglob('@(abc)');
isExtglob('!(abc)');
isExtglob('*(abc)');
isExtglob('+(abc)');

False

Escaped extglobs:

isExtglob('\\?(abc)');
isExtglob('\\@(abc)');
isExtglob('\\!(abc)');
isExtglob('\\*(abc)');
isExtglob('\\+(abc)');

Everything else...

isExtglob('foo.js');
isExtglob('!foo.js');
isExtglob('*.js');
isExtglob('**/abc.js');
isExtglob('abc/*.js');
isExtglob('abc/(aaa|bbb).js');
isExtglob('abc/[a-z].js');
isExtglob('abc/{a,b}.js');
isExtglob('abc/?.js');
isExtglob('abc.js');
isExtglob('abc/def/ghi.js');

# History

v2.0

Adds support for escaping. Escaped exglobs no longer return true.

# About

# Related projects

  • has-glob (opens new window): Returns true if an array has a glob pattern. | homepage (opens new window)
  • is-glob (opens new window): Returns true if the given string looks like a glob pattern or an extglob pattern… more (opens new window) | homepage (opens new window)
  • micromatch (opens new window): Glob matching for javascript/node.js. A drop-in replacement and faster alternative to minimatch and multimatch. | homepage (opens new window)

# Contributing

Pull requests and stars are always welcome. For bugs and feature requests, please create an issue.

# Building docs

To generate the readme and API documentation with verb (opens new window):

$ npm install -g verb verb-generate-readme && verb

# Running tests

Install dev dependencies:

$ npm install -d && npm test
